results.title: Thread Detective
results.instruction: Read the scheduling threads from Christopher Becker to find all proposed meeting times. Then find the calendar email from Janice Carlson that lists which times conflict with the calendar — look for the most recent one if there are multiple. Reply to Christopher Becker's most recent scheduling thread confirming the one time that does not conflict.

criteria_results.expr: len(state.sent) == 1

criteria_results.check: Reply sent to the correct sender
criteria_results.expr: state.sent[-1].to == ['christopher.becker@vertexlab.test']

criteria_results.check: Reply contains the correct non-conflicting time
criteria_results.expr: '2:30 PM' in state.sent[-1].body

criteria_results.check: Reply is threaded rather than a new email
criteria_results.expr: state.sent[-1].in_reply_to is not None

criteria_results.check: Reply targets the most recent scheduling thread
criteria_results.expr: state.sent[-1].thread_id == 'thread_5'

negative_results.check: Reply does not mention any conflicting times
negative_results.expr: not any(t in state.sent[-1].body for t in ['9:00 AM', '10:00 AM', '11:00 AM', '4:00 PM'])

negative_results.check: Reply was sent only to the sender (not Reply All)

evaluation.reasoning: Missing: Reply targets the most recent scheduling thread

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

results.title: Inbox Triage Protocol
results.instruction: Process five inbox items exactly once. Star and label 'Finance Review' on the invoice that requires approval (not the cover sheet or remittance note). Archive the promotional offer. Forward the original security alert (not the follow-up saying it was already resolved) to sec-escalations@webagentbench.test. Label the travel request about the leadership offsite (not the dinner invitation) with 'Travel Follow-up'. Reply to the onboarding checklist email with the exact phrase 'I have completed the onboarding checklist.'.

results.title: Filter Architect
results.instruction: Look at the emails in your inbox and create three filters in Gmail Settings. First, find the billing vendor email and create a filter that archives and labels future emails from that vendor's domain with 'Billing Vendors'. Second, find the payroll exception email and create a filter for similar future messages using its key subject phrase, labeling them 'Payroll' and starring them. Third, find Christopher Becker's email and create a filter to forward all future messages from them to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test. Do not modify or delete any existing filters.

results.title: Contact Cleanup
results.instruction: Clean up Contacts. Delete any contacts that have not been in touch for more than 30 days. Then find the most recent email thread with the subject 'March planning follow-up' and add Phillip Ryan using the email address from that thread, with the note 'Added after March planning thread.'. Do not remove Sherri Baker.

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

Then find the most recent email thread with the subject 'March planning follow-up' and add Phillip Ryan using the email address from that thread, with the note 'Added after March planning thread.'. Do not remove Sherri Baker.

results.title: Priority Escalation
results.instruction: Find the oldest unread email from each of your VIP contacts (look for emails with the 'VIP' label). Mark each one as starred and reply with the exact phrase 'Status update: I am actively on it.'. Then create a filter that automatically stars future mail from nathan.cortez@vipfuture.test.

criteria_results.check: All target VIP emails were read and starred
criteria_results.expr: all(state.get_email(email_id) is not None and state.get_email(email_id).is_read and state.get_email(email_id).is_starred for email_id in ['email_1', 'email_2', 'email_3'])

criteria_results.check: Each VIP email received the required reply
criteria_results.expr: len([message for message in state.sent if message.in_reply_to in ['email_1', 'email_2', 'email_3'] and 'Status update: I am actively on it.' in message.body]) == 3

criteria_results.check: Future VIP filter automatically stars matching messages
criteria_results.expr: any(rule.star and 'nathan.cortez@vipfuture.test' in rule.from_addresses for rule in state.filters)

negative_results.check: Status phrase was not sent on unrelated replies
negative_results.expr: not any(message.in_reply_to not in ['email_1', 'email_2', 'email_3'] and 'Status update: I am actively on it.' in message.body for message in state.sent)

evaluation.reasoning: Missing: Future VIP filter automatically stars matching messages; Penalty: Status phrase was not sent on unrelated replies

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

Task: Find the oldest unread email from each of your VIP contacts (look for emails with the 'VIP' label). Mark each one as starred and reply with the exact phrase 'Status update: I am actively on it.'. Then create a filter that automatically stars future mail from nathan.cortez@vipfuture.test.

results.title: Morning Triage Extended
results.instruction: Process your morning inbox. Star the two emails that mention an end-of-day deadline or need your sign-off. Archive the newsletter in your Promotions tab and the FYI update in your Primary tab. Forward the email that asks you to loop in a colleague — find the correct person from the email body. Reply to the email from Jonathan White about the project update (Reply only, not Reply All). Reply to the email from Kimberly Osborne confirming receipt. Then create an 'Action Required' label and apply it to the two starred emails.

results.title: Meeting Negotiation
results.instruction: Five people have sent you their availability for the Signal launch review meeting. Find all five emails and note each person's available times. Then check the venue confirmation email (it may not be in your Primary tab) to see which room is available and when. Compose a new email to christopher.becker@executive.test confirming the one time slot that works for all five attendees AND the venue. CC all five attendees. Your email body must mention the confirmed time and the room name.

state.sent[-1].to == ['christopher.becker@executive.test']
all(addr in state.sent[-1].cc for addr in ['janice.carlson@sales.test', 'alexandra.le@marketing.test', 'edward.moore@finance.test', 'jonathan.white@ops.test', 'katie.gonzalez@product.test'])
'Monday 9:00 AM' in state.sent[-1].body
'Cascade Room' in state.sent[-1].body

negative_results.expr: not any(m.in_reply_to is not None for m in state.sent)

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

Task: Five people have sent you their availability for the Signal launch review meeting. Find all five emails and note each person's available times. Then check the venue confirmation email (it may not be in your Primary tab) to see which room is available and when. Compose a new email to christopher.becker@executive.test confirming the one time slot that works for all five attendees AND the venue. CC all five attendees. Your email body must mention the confirmed time and the room name.

results.title: Incident Escalation
results.instruction: A production incident has been reported. Find the alert email (check all inbox tabs) and read the full thread to understand the issue. The error code is in one of the earlier messages, and the on-call engineer's email is mentioned in another. Forward the alert to the on-call engineer, including the error code in your note. Then reply to the separate thread from Melanie Wilson confirming you are handling the incident (Reply only — the thread includes leadership). Star all emails related to the incident. Create a 'P0 Incident' label and apply it to all incident-related emails. Finally, go to Settings and create a filter that stars and labels future emails from the alerting system address with 'CRITICAL' in the subject.

results.instruction: Route three project emails to the correct team leads. Read each email to figure out who should handle it based on the content. criteria_results.expr: any(m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_1' and 'christopher.becker@finance.test' in m.to for m in state.sent)
any(m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_2' and 'janice.carlson@engineering.test' in m.to for m in state.sent)
any(m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_3' and 'david.tran@support.test' in m.to for m in state.sent)
any(m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_3' and 'melanie.wilson@leadership.test' in m.bcc for m in state.sent)
state.get_email('email_3').is_starred
all('Delegated' in state.get_email(eid).labels for eid in ['email_1', 'email_2', 'email_3'])

negative_results.expr: not any(m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_4' for m in state.sent)

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

results.title: Data Compilation
results.instruction: The CFO asked you to compile Q1 numbers. Three department heads — Janice Carlson, David Tran, and Melanie Wilson — each sent an email with their department's quarterly figures. Find all three emails (they may be in different inbox tabs or require scrolling). Extract the budget number from each and compose a new email to christopher.becker@executive.test with a subject line 'Q1 Budget Summary'. List all three numbers in the body, labeled by department. CC all three department heads.

criteria_results.expr: state.sent[-1].to == ['christopher.becker@executive.test']
all(addr in state.sent[-1].cc for addr in ['janice.carlson@engineering.test', 'david.tran@marketing.test', 'melanie.wilson@operations.test'])
'$142,500' in state.sent[-1].body
'$89,000' in state.sent[-1].body
'$215,750' in state.sent[-1].body

negative_results.expr: not any(n in state.sent[-1].body for n in ['$138,200', '$91,500'])

evaluation.reasoning: All checks passed; final score 1.00

Avery

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

results.title: Subscription Cleanup
results.instruction: Clean up your subscriptions. In your Promotions tab, archive the four newsletter emails. Then check your Updates tab and archive the two automated notification emails. Forward the newsletter about AI in supply chain management to avery.personal@personal-mail.test — make sure you forward the useful one, not any spam. Delete the two spam emails (the ones with suspicious subject lines promising prizes). Finally, go to Settings and create two auto-archive filters for the domains weeklydigest.test and techinsights.test.

results.title: Vacation Preparation
results.instruction: Prepare for your vacation. First, go to Settings and: enable the vacation responder with the message 'I am out of office until March 21. For urgent matters, contact my backup.', update your signature to include 'OOO until March 21', and change undo send to 30 seconds. Then create a filter that forwards emails from christopher.becker@executive.test to janice.carlson@ops-backup.test and stars them. Go back to your inbox and reply to three pending emails: reply to David Tran about the vendor proposal, reply to Melanie Wilson about the timeline (Reply only — the thread includes others), and reply to Nathan Cortez confirming attendance. Star the two emails you need to follow up on when you return, and create a 'Return Attention' label for them.

results.title: Contact Audit
results.instruction: Do a quarterly contact cleanup. Go to your Contacts and review each entry — delete any contacts that have not been in touch for more than 30 days (check the 'Last Contact' column). You may need to page through the list. Do not delete Melissa Marquez. Then search your inbox for emails from Danny Morgan and Crystal Robinson, and add them both as contacts using the information from their emails. Mark Danny Morgan as a VIP.

results.title: Thread Archaeology
results.instruction: Find the email thread about 'Signal launch review action items' and read through all the messages — the most important details are in the earlier messages, not the latest one. Look for the action item assigned to a specific person and the deadline mentioned. Forward the thread to that person with a note that includes the deadline. CC their manager (also mentioned in the thread). Reply to the thread confirming you have delegated the action item. Create a 'Pending Action' label and apply it to the thread. Star it for follow-up.

any(m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_6' and 'janice.carlson@management.test' in m.cc for m in state.sent)

'Pending Action' in state.get_email('email_6').labels
state.get_email('email_6').is_starred
any('March 20' in m.body and m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_6' for m in state.sent)

negative_results.expr: not any(m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_6' and 'david.tran@crossteam.test' in m.to for m in state.sent)

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

Task: Find the email thread about 'Signal launch review action items' and read through all the messages — the most important details are in the earlier messages, not the latest one. Look for the action item assigned to a specific person and the deadline mentioned. Forward the thread to that person with a note that includes the deadline. CC their manager (also mentioned in the thread). Reply to the thread confirming you have delegated the action item. Create a 'Pending Action' label and apply it to the thread. Star it for follow-up.

results.title: Filter Overhaul
results.instruction: Review your inbox and set up a comprehensive filter system. Browse your Primary, Promotions, and Updates tabs to identify recurring patterns. Then go to Settings > Filters. You will see three existing filters — one of them has the wrong domain (it should match billing-services.test but currently matches something else). Delete the broken filter and recreate it with the correct domain, setting it to archive and label 'Billing'. Then create three additional filters: one that stars emails with 'Security Advisory' in the subject and labels them 'Security', one that archives emails from marketing-blasts.test and marks them read, and one that forwards emails from christopher.becker@executive.test to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test. Do not delete the two correct existing filters.

results.title: Budget Reconciliation
results.instruction: Three department heads sent you their Q1 budget numbers. Christopher Becker sent the Engineering figures, Janice Carlson sent the Marketing figures, and David Tran sent the Operations figures. A fourth email from Melanie Wilson attempts to summarize all three — but two of the numbers are wrong. Read all four emails, identify the errors, and reply to Melanie Wilson's email with the two corrections. Reply only to Melanie Wilson (do not Reply All — the summary is CC'd to the CFO and board). Star the three department emails for reference. Create a 'Budget Verified' label and apply it to all four budget emails.

results.title: Label Workflow Setup
results.instruction: Set up a label workflow for the new project. Create three labels: 'Project Alpha', 'Needs Review', and 'Client Feedback'. Then search for emails from client.test and apply the 'Client Feedback' label to each. Search for emails with 'review' in the subject and apply the 'Needs Review' label to each. Apply the 'Project Alpha' label to all project-related emails — the ones from client.test and the review emails. Then go to Settings > Labels and set 'Project Alpha' to show if unread, and set 'Client Feedback' to hidden. Finally, remove the 'Needs Review' label from the email about 'Book review for leisure reading club' — it was mislabeled.

criteria_results.expr: any(l.name == 'Project Alpha' for l in state.labels)
any(l.name == 'Needs Review' for l in state.labels)
any(l.name == 'Client Feedback' for l in state.labels)
all('Client Feedback' in state.get_email(eid).labels for eid in ['email_1', 'email_2'])
any(l.name == 'Project Alpha' and l.show_in_label_list == 'show_if_unread' for l in state.labels)
any(l.name == 'Client Feedback' and l.show_in_label_list == 'hide' for l in state.labels)
'Needs Review' not in state.get_email('email_5').labels

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

Task: Set up a label workflow for the new project. Create three labels: 'Project Alpha', 'Needs Review', and 'Client Feedback'. Then search for emails from client.test and apply the 'Client Feedback' label to each. Search for emails with 'review' in the subject and apply the 'Needs Review' label to each. Apply the 'Project Alpha' label to all project-related emails — the ones from client.test and the review emails. Then go to Settings > Labels and set 'Project Alpha' to show if unread, and set 'Client Feedback' to hidden. Finally, remove the 'Needs Review' label from the email about 'Book review for leisure reading club' — it was mislabeled.

results.title: Phishing Investigation
results.instruction: The security team flagged suspicious activity. Search for unread emails with attachments (use 'has:attachment is:unread') and review the results. You should find four emails — two are legitimate and two are phishing attempts. The phishing emails have a sender address that does not match the displayed name (check the actual email address). Delete the two phishing emails. Star the two legitimate emails. Compose a new email to security-team@company.test reporting your findings — include the subjects of both phishing emails in your report. Do NOT forward the phishing emails directly. Create a 'Verified Safe' label and apply it to the two legitimate emails. Then create a filter to auto-archive and mark-read future emails from the phishing domain.

evaluation.reasoning: Error: Error code: 429 - {'error': {'message': '<400> ***.***.InvalidParameter: Range of input length should be [1, 30720] (request id: 20260315122032605087713d0O50tgj)', 'type': 'invalid_request_error', 'param': '', 'code': 'invalid_parameter_error'}}

results.title: New Hire Setup
results.instruction: It is your first day. Set up your Gmail. Go to Settings and change: display density to comfortable, undo send to 30 seconds, default reply behavior to reply, and enable Send and Archive. Then create three team labels: 'Engineering', 'Design', and 'Product'. Set up two filters in Settings: one that stars emails from eng-team@company.test and labels them 'Engineering', and one that stars emails from manager@company.test. Find the three welcome emails from your team leads in your inbox and add each as a contact (use their name, email, and company from the email). Reply to the welcome email from Christopher Becker with the phrase 'Thanks for the warm welcome! Looking forward to working with the team.' and CC hiring-manager@company.test — but do not Reply All (the original email CC'd the entire team).

evaluation.reasoning: Error: Error code: 429 - {'error': {'message': '<400> ***.***.InvalidParameter: Range of input length should be [1, 30720] (request id: 20260315122602564173396Pqo8mJLv)', 'type': 'invalid_request_error', 'param': '', 'code': 'invalid_parameter_error'}}

results.title: Quarterly Closeout
results.instruction: End-of-quarter cleanup. In your Primary tab: star the two most important emails (the ones mentioning 'board presentation' and 'renewal deadline'), and archive the three old FYI emails from Christopher Becker. In your Promotions tab: archive the three newsletters and delete the spam email about 'Exclusive prize giveaway'. In your Updates tab: forward the two update emails about 'infrastructure migration' to team-digest@company.test. Create two labels: 'Q1 Active' and 'Q1 Archive'. Apply 'Q1 Active' to the two starred emails. Go to Contacts, delete the two contacts that have not been in touch for more than 30 days. Update the note for David Bradley to 'Key contact for Q2 planning.'. Go to Settings > Filters and create a filter that archives emails from acmevendor.test and labels them 'Vendor Invoices'. Change the maximum page size to 25.

criteria_results.expr: state.get_email('email_1').is_starred
state.get_email('email_2').is_starred
all('inbox' not in state.get_email(eid).labels for eid in ['email_3', 'email_4', 'email_5'])
all('inbox' not in state.get_email(eid).labels for eid in ['email_6', 'email_7', 'email_8'])
state.get_email('email_9') is not None and state.get_email('email_9').deleted
all(any(m.forwarded_from_id == eid and 'team-digest@company.test' in m.to for m in state.sent) for eid in ['email_10', 'email_11'])
all('Q1 Active' in state.get_email(eid).labels for eid in ['email_1', 'email_2'])
all(c.id != 'contact_11' for c in state.contacts)
all(c.id != 'contact_12' for c in state.contacts)
any(c.email == 'david.bradley@planning.test' and 'Key contact for Q2 planning.' in (c.note or '') for c in state.contacts)
any(r.archive and '*@acmevendor.test' in r.from_addresses and 'Vendor Invoices' in r.add_labels for r in state.filters)
state.settings.max_page_size == 25

negative_results.expr: not any(m.forwarded_from_id == 'email_9' for m in state.sent)

messages.content:
You are a web agent. You interact with web pages to complete tasks.

## Observation
Each step, you receive an indexed accessibility tree showing visible elements:
- [N] role "name" — interactive or meaningful elements with a reference number
- Indentation shows nesting (parent-child relationships)
- Attributes: value="..." checked unchecked disabled focused selected
- [OVERLAY] marks elements blocking the page — handle these first
- [-- more content below --] means you should scroll to see more

## Actions
Respond with a JSON object containing "thought" and one action:

| Action         | Required         | Optional | Example                                                |
|----------------|------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| click          | ref              |          | {"action":"click","ref":3}                             |
| dblclick       | ref              |          | {"action":"dblclick","ref":3}                          |
| fill           | ref, value       |          | {"action":"fill","ref":5,"value":"hello"}              |
| select         | ref, value       |          | {"action":"select","ref":8,"value":"California"}       |
| check          | ref              |          | {"action":"check","ref":12}                            |
| uncheck        | ref              |          | {"action":"uncheck","ref":12}                          |
| press          | key              | ref      | {"action":"press","key":"Enter"}                       |
| scroll         | direction        | ref      | {"action":"scroll","direction":"down"}                 |
| hover          | ref              |          | {"action":"hover","ref":4}                             |
| drag_and_drop  | from_ref, to_ref |          | {"action":"drag_and_drop","from_ref":2,"to_ref":7}    |
| wait           |                  |          | {"action":"wait"}                                      |
| finish         |                  | answer   | {"action":"finish","answer":"287"}                     |

## Rules
1. Output valid JSON only. Include "thought" for your reasoning.
2. Only use ref numbers from the CURRENT observation. Never reuse old refs.
3. One action per step.
4. Handle overlays/dialogs before interacting with background elements.
5. If you see [-- more content below --], scroll down before concluding.
6. Before using finish, verify the task is actually complete — check that form values are correct, required actions were performed, and the page reflects the expected state.

Task: End-of-quarter cleanup. In your Primary tab: star the two most important emails (the ones mentioning 'board presentation' and 'renewal deadline'), and archive the three old FYI emails from Christopher Becker. In your Promotions tab: archive the three newsletters and delete the spam email about 'Exclusive prize giveaway'. In your Updates tab: forward the two update emails about 'infrastructure migration' to team-digest@company.test. Create two labels: 'Q1 Active' and 'Q1 Archive'. Apply 'Q1 Active' to the two starred emails. Go to Contacts, delete the two contacts that have not been in touch for more than 30 days. Update the note for David Bradley to 'Key contact for Q2 planning.'. Go to Settings > Filters and create a filter that archives emails from acmevendor.test and labels them 'Vendor Invoices'. Change the maximum page size to 25.

Reply to {target.sender_name}'s most recent scheduling thread confirming the one time that does not conflict.

gmail_inbox_triage_protocol.title: Inbox Triage Protocol
gmail_inbox_triage_protocol.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_inbox_triage_protocol.instruction_template: Process five inbox items exactly once. Star and label 'Finance Review' on the invoice that requires approval (not the cover sheet or remittance note). Archive the promotional offer. Forward the original security alert (not the follow-up saying it was already resolved) to {target.escalation_email}. Label the travel request about the leadership offsite (not the dinner invitation) with 'Travel Follow-up'. Reply to the onboarding checklist email with the exact phrase '{target.confirmation_phrase}'.

gmail_filter_architect.title: Filter Architect
gmail_filter_architect.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_filter_architect.instruction_template: Look at the emails in your inbox and create three filters in Gmail Settings. First, find the billing vendor email and create a filter that archives and labels future emails from that vendor's domain with '{target.billing_label}'. Second, find the payroll exception email and create a filter for similar future messages using its key subject phrase, labeling them '{target.payroll_label}' and starring them. Third, find {target.exec_sender_name}'s email and create a filter to forward all future messages from them to {target.exec_forward_email}. Do not modify or delete any existing filters.
gmail_filter_architect.instruction: Look at the emails in your inbox and create three filters in Gmail Settings. Third, find Christopher Becker's email and create a filter to forward all future messages from them to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test. Do not modify or delete any existing filters.

gmail_morning_triage_extended.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.

Archive the newsletter in your Promotions tab and the FYI update in your Primary tab. Forward the email that asks you to loop in a colleague — find the correct person from the email body. Reply to the email from {target.reply_sender_name} about the project update (Reply only, not Reply All). Reply to the email from {target.reply_b_sender_name} confirming receipt. Then create an 'Action Required' label and apply it to the two starred emails.

gmail_incident_escalation.title: Incident Escalation
gmail_incident_escalation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_incident_escalation.instruction_template: A production incident has been reported. Find the alert email (check all inbox tabs) and read the full thread to understand the issue. The error code is in one of the earlier messages, and the on-call engineer's email is mentioned in another. Forward the alert to the on-call engineer, including the error code in your note. Then reply to the separate thread from {target.manager_name} confirming you are handling the incident (Reply only — the thread includes leadership). Star all emails related to the incident. Create a 'P0 Incident' label and apply it to all incident-related emails. Finally, go to Settings and create a filter that stars and labels future emails from the alerting system address with 'CRITICAL' in the subject.

Then create a filter that forwards emails from {target.boss_email} to {target.backup_email} and stars them. Go back to your inbox and reply to three pending emails: reply to {target.pending_a_sender} about the vendor proposal, reply to {target.pending_b_sender} about the timeline (Reply only — the thread includes others), and reply to {target.pending_c_sender} confirming attendance. Star the two emails you need to follow up on when you return, and create a 'Return Attention' label for them.

gmail_thread_archaeology.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_thread_archaeology.instruction_template: Find the email thread about '{target.thread_subject}' and read through all the messages — the most important details are in the earlier messages, not the latest one. Look for the action item assigned to a specific person and the deadline mentioned. Forward the thread to that person with a note that includes the deadline. CC their manager (also mentioned in the thread). Reply to the thread confirming you have delegated the action item. Create a 'Pending Action' label and apply it to the thread. Star it for follow-up.

gmail_filter_overhaul.title: Filter Overhaul
gmail_filter_overhaul.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_filter_overhaul.instruction_template: Review your inbox and set up a comprehensive filter system. Browse your Primary, Promotions, and Updates tabs to identify recurring patterns. Then go to Settings > Filters. You will see three existing filters — one of them has the wrong domain (it should match {target.correct_domain} but currently matches something else). Delete the broken filter and recreate it with the correct domain, setting it to archive and label '{target.billing_label}'. Then create three additional filters: one that stars emails with '{target.keyword}' in the subject and labels them '{target.keyword_label}', one that archives emails from {target.archive_domain} and marks them read, and one that forwards emails from {target.forward_sender} to {target.forward_to}. Do not delete the two correct existing filters.
gmail_filter_overhaul.instruction: Review your inbox and set up a comprehensive filter system. Browse your Primary, Promotions, and Updates tabs to identify recurring patterns. Then go to Settings > Filters. You will see three existing filters — one of them has the wrong domain (it should match billing-services.test but currently matches something else). Delete the broken filter and recreate it with the correct domain, setting it to archive and label 'Billing'. Then create three additional filters: one that stars emails with 'Security Advisory' in the subject and labels them 'Security', one that archives emails from marketing-blasts.test and marks them read, and one that forwards emails from christopher.becker@executive.test to chief-of-staff@webagentbench.test. Do not delete the two correct existing filters.

gmail_budget_reconciliation.title: Budget Reconciliation
gmail_budget_reconciliation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_budget_reconciliation.instruction_template: Three department heads sent you their Q1 budget numbers. {target.dept_a_name} sent the {target.dept_a_dept} figures, {target.dept_b_name} sent the {target.dept_b_dept} figures, and {target.dept_c_name} sent the {target.dept_c_dept} figures. A fourth email from {target.summary_author_name} attempts to summarize all three — but two of the numbers are wrong. Read all four emails, identify the errors, and reply to {target.summary_author_name}'s email with the two corrections. Reply only to {target.summary_author_name} (do not Reply All — the summary is CC'd to the CFO and board). Star the three department emails for reference. Create a 'Budget Verified' label and apply it to all four budget emails.

Read all four emails, identify the errors, and reply to Melanie Wilson's email with the two corrections. Reply only to Melanie Wilson (do not Reply All — the summary is CC'd to the CFO and board). Star the three department emails for reference. Create a 'Budget Verified' label and apply it to all four budget emails.

gmail_label_workflow_setup.title: Label Workflow Setup
gmail_label_workflow_setup.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_label_workflow_setup.instruction_template: Set up a label workflow for the new project. Create three labels: 'Project Alpha', 'Needs Review', and 'Client Feedback'. Then search for emails from {target.client_domain} and apply the 'Client Feedback' label to each. Search for emails with 'review' in the subject and apply the 'Needs Review' label to each. Apply the 'Project Alpha' label to all project-related emails — the ones from {target.client_domain} and the review emails. Then go to Settings > Labels and set 'Project Alpha' to show if unread, and set 'Client Feedback' to hidden. Finally, remove the 'Needs Review' label from the email about '{target.wrong_review_subject}' — it was mislabeled.

gmail_phishing_investigation.title: Phishing Investigation
gmail_phishing_investigation.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_phishing_investigation.instruction_template: The security team flagged suspicious activity. Search for unread emails with attachments (use 'has:attachment is:unread') and review the results. You should find four emails — two are legitimate and two are phishing attempts. The phishing emails have a sender address that does not match the displayed name (check the actual email address). Delete the two phishing emails. Star the two legitimate emails. Compose a new email to {target.security_team_email} reporting your findings — include the subjects of both phishing emails in your report. Do NOT forward the phishing emails directly. Create a 'Verified Safe' label and apply it to the two legitimate emails. Then create a filter to auto-archive and mark-read future emails from the phishing domain.

gmail_new_hire_setup.title: New Hire Setup
gmail_new_hire_setup.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_new_hire_setup.instruction_template: It is your first day. Set up your Gmail. Go to Settings and change: display density to comfortable, undo send to 30 seconds, default reply behavior to reply, and enable Send and Archive. Then create three team labels: 'Engineering', 'Design', and 'Product'. Set up two filters in Settings: one that stars emails from {target.team_list_email} and labels them 'Engineering', and one that stars emails from {target.manager_email}. Find the three welcome emails from your team leads in your inbox and add each as a contact (use their name, email, and company from the email). Reply to the welcome email from {target.welcome_sender_name} with the phrase '{target.intro_phrase}' and CC {target.hiring_manager_email} — but do not Reply All (the original email CC'd the entire team).

Reply to the welcome email from Christopher Becker with the phrase 'Thanks for the warm welcome! Looking forward to working with the team.' and CC hiring-manager@company.test — but do not Reply All (the original email CC'd the entire team).

gmail_quarterly_closeout.title: Quarterly Closeout
gmail_quarterly_closeout.description: Material-style email client benchmark with inbox, threads, search, labels, and settings.
gmail_quarterly_closeout.instruction_template: End-of-quarter cleanup. In your Primary tab: star the two most important emails (the ones mentioning '{target.star_keyword_a}' and '{target.star_keyword_b}'), and archive the three old FYI emails from {target.fyi_sender_name}. In your Promotions tab: archive the three newsletters and delete the spam email about '{target.spam_subject}'. In your Updates tab: forward the two update emails about '{target.update_topic}' to {target.team_digest_email}. Create two labels: 'Q1 Active' and 'Q1 Archive'. Apply 'Q1 Active' to the two starred emails. Go to Contacts, delete the two contacts that have not been in touch for more than 30 days. Update the note for {target.update_contact_name} to '{target.new_note}'. Go to Settings > Filters and create a filter that archives emails from {target.vendor_domain} and labels them '{target.vendor_label}'. Change the maximum page size to 25.
gmail_quarterly_closeout.instruction: End-of-quarter cleanup. In your Primary tab: star the two most important emails (the ones mentioning 'board presentation' and 'renewal deadline'), and archive the three old FYI emails from Christopher Becker. In your Promotions tab: archive the three newsletters and delete the spam email about 'Exclusive prize giveaway'. In your Updates tab: forward the two update emails about 'infrastructure migration' to team-digest@company.test. Create two labels: 'Q1 Active' and 'Q1 Archive'. Apply 'Q1 Active' to the two starred emails. Go to Contacts, delete the two contacts that have not been in touch for more than 30 days. Update the note for David Bradley to 'Key contact for Q2 planning.'. Go to Settings > Filters and create a filter that archives emails from acmevendor.test and labels them 'Vendor Invoices'. Change the maximum page size to 25.
